rationale: The cited paper is thematically close to the citing paper’s marketing studies: both examine how auditory and visual elements jointly shape ad processing outcomes, including recall. The citing note explicitly includes message recall and consumer response effects, while the cited note reports script-dependent auditory/visual interference and retrieval-cue effects on ad-copy recall and attitudes. Given this substantive overlap, this is not clearly an incidental mention and should be kept for full relation judgment.
